item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very fun article. I started really liking it when you mentioned the many types of buildings the chickens can manifest as. The absurdity of a stadium chicken/Cathedral chicken is absolutely delightful.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know how to repeatedly reference a single footnote in MediaWiki, but Wikidot doesn't use MediaWiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VERY tiny nitpick: I do not believe that footnote 5 is required as it is identical to footnote 4. Just reference footnote 4 again in place of 5.

Otherwise weird idea. Grow you own house - what's not to like?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These critters are based on Russian mythology. What better characteristically-Russian artifact would you suggest?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I for one really liked this. Yes, it is straightforward. Yes, this is practically everything I expected it to be right out the gate and not many punches were pulled. However, yes, what I expected was really good. This is not my strongest upvote, but these things are damn cute, and I want one, and that makes me upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too straightforward an application of the idea, and linking to 352 does you no favors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is funny but a little dense/verbose.

I think it might benefit from a graphic, if that's even producible.

The major question I have is why have a Matryoshka doll as a catalyst? I was expecting nested-eggs but at the end of it,

"The breed(s) of the parents does not seem to play a part: for example, when hen 3168-F-417 is bred to rooster 3168-M-131, the offspring have consistently been 75% split-level ranch, while the offspring of that same hen with rooster 3168-M-132 have consistently been 75% Pacific lodge, even though all three birds are purebred Orpington chickens."

had me laughing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
when purified, this hormone can induce ectoentropically rapid growth in non-anomalous chickens and in architectural structures.

This is where you won me over - doubly so for not addressing this outside that one line, and leaving it to the reader's imagination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you intentionally place this below "finna nut" in the mainlist so it would rhyme? :-P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm fairly certain you don't need footnotes to clarify what a rooster and a hen are. The little house chickens aren't really interesting enough for an up vote, but the article is well written enough to avoid a down vote. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The initial inspiration for this SCP was the authentically-chickenlike behavior of Baba Yaga's House in Summer in Orcus, by Ursula K. Vernon.

You can do a lot more tests on anomalous organisms if you can produce them pretty much at will.
